notes: This batch reopens the axis0 trajectory-correlation v2 orphan as its own bounded result-only family.
Source membership is bounded to results_axis0_traj_corr_suite_v2.json and does not merge in the earlier local family or the repo-top descendants.
possible_downstream_consequence_policy: possible downstream consequence only; no direct A2-1 or A1 promotion from this batch

comparison_anchors.relevance: Provides continuity from the previous result-only orphan pass and records the explicit decision to keep the current surface separate.
Provides the earlier local trajectory-correlation family anchor for contract comparison.
Shows the earlier local full-absolute 32-case trajectory-correlation surface.
Shows the repo-top compressed single-sequence descendant contract.
Shows the repo-top compressed two-sequence entangled-init delta descendant contract.
Shows catalog listing for the current orphan and the earlier local family.
Shows zero repo-held evidence-pack visibility for the current orphan.

runtime_expectations: The current surface stores 128 axis0 trajectory entries at trials 128, cycles 64, theta 0.07, with four sequences, a hidden T1/T2 axis, two directions, two init modes, two gate types, and two repetition labels.
Absolute baseline entries are only for SEQ01; SEQ02 through SEQ04 are stored as deltas against the corresponding prefix baseline.
No direct runner-name anchor is recoverable in the current simpy inventory.
The next remaining result-only orphan begins at results_ultra3_full_geometry_stage16_axis0.json.
failure_modes: Inventing a runner anchor that is not present.
Treating missing absolute SEQ02-04 entries as absent runs instead of delta encoding.
Collapsing the current orphan into the earlier local suite or into repo-top V4/V5.
Ignoring the hidden T1/T2 axis in the key lattice.
Mistaking catalog presence for evidence admission.

sims_split_rule.notes: This batch remains inside the separate sims source class and does not mix with ordinary theory/doc extraction.
